name: spring-boot
description: Spring Boot framework power pack — Spring Security review, JPA N+1 detection, transaction boundaries, configuration hygiene, and actuator hardening
rules: Use constructor injection — never @Autowired on fields
Annotate service-layer methods with @Transactional; never on @Controller
Use @ConfigurationProperties + Records for typed config — never @Value strings everywhere
JPA: use fetch joins / @EntityGraph to prevent N+1 queries
Lock down actuator endpoints — never expose /actuator/* publicly
Use ResponseEntity<T> with explicit status codes; document with @Operation
Prefer record DTOs over Lombok @Data on the API boundary
Configure CORS at SecurityFilterChain — not via @CrossOrigin sprinkled on controllers

checks.description: @Autowired on fields — use constructor injection instead (testability, immutability)
permitAll() in SecurityFilterChain on broad patterns can expose endpoints
csrf().disable() — only acceptable for stateless APIs; verify intent
spring.jpa.show-sql=true logs every query — disable in production
spring.jpa.open-in-view defaults true — causes N+1 and lazy-load surprises in views
spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto=create/create-drop wipes data on restart — never in prod
Hardcoded password in application properties — use env var ${VAR}
@Transactional on private/protected method — proxy ignores, no transaction applied
BCryptPasswordEncoder strength < 10 is too weak for production
catch (Exception e) with no rethrow swallows business errors
